content.assessment: This proposal operates through governance levers already demonstrated to be functional within the current institutional order: ministerial discretionary approval power has been exercised to deny track-expansion requests explicitly on segregation grounds (F-live-hungarian_education_system-09), and the sitting minister has already floated demographic decline as grounds for reviewing (though not abolishing) the selective system (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10). This gives the mechanism unusually strong institutional precedent compared to the other proposals. However, contraction via score thresholds and capacity caps does not touch the admission mechanism itself (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02) or the statutory free-choice right (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03), so the sorting logic that produces documented parental-education skew (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) and coaching investment (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05) persists among a smaller pool of remaining places.
opportunities: Systematic extension of the Tiszavasvári-style discretionary denial precedent (F-live-hungarian_education_system-09) could institutionalize a segregation-aware approval standard across the network
Demographic framing already used by the minister (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10) offers a politically low-friction justification independent of equity ideology
strengths: Builds directly on documented, already-exercised ministerial discretionary power to block segregation-reinforcing track changes (F-live-hungarian_education_system-09)

Aligns with the minister's own stated rationale that declining demographics undermine justification for the current selective system, providing institutional legitimacy cover (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10)
threats: Reducing state-sector capacity without altering admission criteria could concentrate the same parental-education composition skew (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) into a scarcer set of remaining places, intensifying rather than easing sorting pressure per seat

The minister's own framing that 6/8-grade results reflect family background rather than value-added learning (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08) implies that shrinking capacity does not resolve the diagnosed cause, only its scale
weaknesses: Leaves the centralized admission exam and its coaching-sensitive design entirely intact (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02, F-live-hungarian_education_system-05)
Does not engage the free-choice statutory guarantee (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03), so families retain full latitude to pursue remaining scarce selective seats via the same channels
